title: Dfg: resolve multi-driven signal ranges
body:
Follow up from #3740, this adds a warning and resolution of multi-driven bit ranges in DFG.

Questions:
- Are you happy reusing the MULTIDRIVEN warning?
- Is the resolution OK? (see below)

The 'first' driver will be used, and later ones ignored, where first is either: the driver that drives from a lower LSB, or the driver with a smaller source location if the LSBs equal.

Notably:

assign a[8:1] = ..
assign a[9:0] = ... // This applies over the above as 0 < 1

As this is an error I think any resolution is OK. The reason we resolve this rather than leave it be is twofold:
- We want well-formed input to the rest of the passes
- We run DFG twice (pre and post inline), so we would get the MULTIDRIVEN warnings twice without resolution. I don't want to disable them on the first encounter though as we might discover something new post inlining.
